headline: Choose the lane that matches how you want to buy and deploy
subheadline: The pricing system is now designed to route software buyers into checkout quickly, service buyers into implementation briefs, and enterprise buyers into qualified rollout review.
stepsHeadline: Each lane is built for a different buying style

steps.title: Buy Operator OS
steps.description: Best when you are ready for direct software access and a clean product-led path.

steps.title: Submit Launch Sprint
steps.description: Best when you want premium setup help, faster activation, and guided implementation.

steps.title: Scope Enterprise Deployment
steps.description: Best when rollout, governance, hosting, or approvals need to be reviewed first.

tiers.name: Starter
tiers.description: Explore the system architecture and evaluate the workflow before moving deeper.

tiers.name: Operator OS
tiers.description: The core recurring software offer for teams that want the command center itself.

tiers.name: Launch Sprint
tiers.description: A done-with-you implementation package for teams that want the first system built with them.

tiers.name: Enterprise Deployment
tiers.description: For teams that need governed rollout, deployment support, and custom operational scope.

sections.title: Checkout only appears when the route is truly available
sections.description: If Stripe is ready it appears. If PayPal API credentials are absent, PayPal can still route through mr.jwswain@gmail.com.

sections.title: Lead-form-first keeps premium work scoped properly
sections.description: Launch Sprint is for buyers who need an expert-guided setup path instead of a blank dashboard.

sections.title: Qualification-first protects larger deployment conversations
sections.description: Enterprise buyers should not be forced into a low-context checkout path that ignores rollout requirements.

faq.question: Can I still use PayPal if the full PayPal API setup is not live?
faq.answer: Yes. The system now supports a PayPal email routing fallback through mr.jwswain@gmail.com.

faq.question: Why keep services and enterprise next to software?
faq.answer: Because they support different buyer needs and make the overall business more resilient, premium, and believable.

cta.heading: Use pricing to move into the right commercial lane without wasting time.
cta.body: The redesigned pricing page is meant to speed up decision-making, not create more navigation overhead.
